betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Itemduct§f

§aTransport items between inventories through a routed pipe network with shortest-path routing!§f

§bItemducts§f carry items from block to block. Items move at §ehalf a block per second§f (§e40 ticks per block§f) and route to the nearest valid destination. An itemduct rejects items if nowhere can accept them.

§6Crafting§f:
§b• Transparent§f: §e1× Tin Ingot§f + §e8× Hardened Glass§f → §e12× Itemduct§f.
§b• Opaque§f: §e1× Tin Ingot§f + §e1× Lead Ingot§f → §e6× Itemduct (Opaque)§f.

§6Routing Behavior§f:
Items always take the §bshortest path§f through the network. Multiple destinations split the load evenly by default — you can manipulate routing priority with §bVacuum Itemduct§f (boost priority) and §bDense Itemduct§f (lower priority).

§eTip§f: Itemducts route by shortest path. Use §bDense§f and §bVacuum§f variants to manipulate routing priority.
